thread.title: 16 days of activism: NHRC, others demand protection of human rights
thread.title_full: 16 days of activism: NHRC, others demand protection of human rights

author: Frank Ikpefan Abuja
title: 16 days of activism: NHRC, others demand protection of human rights
text:
As part of activities to mark this year’s 16 days of activism against gender-based violence , the National Human Rights Commission and the Rule of Law and Anti-Corruption (RoLAC) have called for the protection of rights of every individual in Nigeria and beyond.
Addressing a press conference in Abuja at the weekend, Executive Secretary of the National Human Rights Commission, Dr Tony Ojukwu SAN, emphasised the significance of this year’s theme, “Equality – Reducing Inequality and Advancing Human Rights.”
Ojukwu noted that the protection of human rights required a collective commitment from everyone.
The Executive Secretary said the programme called for the attention, collaboration and investment of key stakeholders like the media, faith-based organisations, human rights actors, students unions, women associations and security agencies to restrategise in stemming the tide against violence on women.
The event was organised in partnership with RoLAC, funded by the European Union and implemented by International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA).
This year’s campaign is particularly crucial in Nigeria, where gender-based violence remains a pervasive issue.
